rationale: Both papers study physician-directed pharmaceutical detailing in U.S. prescription drug categories and model its effect on prescriptions, suggesting the 2005 paper is a plausible foundational/theory source rather than an incidental mention. The citing paper’s design explicitly uses revealed detailing/prescription data and structural demand modeling, which aligns with the cited paper’s structural Bayesian-learning/detailing framework. Given this strong construct/context overlap (and a confirmed DOI match), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
